audit_status.source_base: Hebrew Exodus / Shemot covenant ordinances text tradition, draft aligned to public-domain comparison layer later
audit_status.review_note: First-pass draft reviewed for justice speech, enemy animal help, poor justice, sojourner love-memory, Sabbath year/week, festivals, messenger, land promise and displacement language with audit safeguards.

verses.restored: You shall not lift/carry a false report. Do not set your hand with wicked one to be violent witness.
You shall not follow many toward harm, and you shall not answer in dispute by bending after many to bend justice.
Nor shall you favor a poor one in his dispute.
When you meet your enemy's ox or donkey wandering, returning, you shall return it to him.
When you see donkey of one hating you lying under its burden, and you would refrain from leaving it to him, releasing, you shall release it with him.
You shall not bend justice of your poor in his dispute.
Keep far from false matter; innocent and righteous/just do not kill, for I will not justify a wicked one.
You shall not take a bribe, for bribe blinds the seeing and twists words of righteous/just ones.
A sojourner you shall not oppress; you yourselves know soul/life of sojourner, for you were sojourners in land of Egypt.
Six years you shall sow your land and gather its produce.
But seventh you shall let it rest and lie fallow, and poor of your people shall eat; what they leave, beast of field shall eat. Thus you shall do to your vineyard and olive grove.
Six days you shall do your work, but on seventh day you shall cease, so your ox and donkey may rest, and son of your female servant and sojourner may be refreshed.
In all that I have said to you, be guarded; name of other gods you shall not mention; it shall not be heard upon your mouth.
Three times you shall hold festival to me in the year.

Festival of Unleavened Bread you shall keep: seven days you shall eat unleavened bread as I commanded you, at appointed time in month of Aviv, for in it you came out from Egypt; none shall appear before my face empty.
And Festival of Harvest, firstfruits of your works, what you sow in field; and Festival of Ingathering at going out of year, when you gather your works from field.
Three times in year all your males shall appear before face of Lord YHWH.
You shall not sacrifice blood/life of my sacrifice with leavened bread; fat of my festival shall not remain until morning.
Beginning of firstfruits of your ground you shall bring to house of YHWH your Divine. You shall not boil a kid in milk of its mother.
Look, I am sending a messenger before you to guard you on the way and to bring you to place that I prepared.
Guard yourself before him and listen to his voice; do not rebel against him, for he will not carry your transgression, for my name is in him.
But if listening, you listen to his voice and do all that I speak, then I will be enemy to your enemies and adversary to your adversaries.
For my messenger will go before you and bring you to Amorite, Hittite, Perizzite, Canaanite, Hivite, and Jebusite; and I will cut them off.
You shall not bow down to their gods, nor serve them, nor do according to their works; but tearing down, you shall tear them down, and shattering, you shall shatter their standing stones.
You shall serve/worship YHWH your Divine, and he will bless your bread and your water; and I will turn aside sickness from your midst.
There shall not be one miscarrying or barren in your land; number of your days I will fill.
My terror I will send before you, and I will confuse all the people to whom you come, and I will give all your enemies to you as turned neck.
I will send hornet before you, and it will drive out Hivite, Canaanite, and Hittite from before you.
I will not drive them out from before you in one year, lest the land become desolate and beast of field multiply against you.
Little by little I will drive them out from before you, until you are fruitful and inherit the land.
I will set your border from Sea of Reeds to Sea of Philistines, and from wilderness to River; for I will give inhabitants of land into your hand, and you shall drive them out from before you.
You shall not cut covenant with them or with their gods.
They shall not dwell in your land, lest they make you sin/misalign against me; for if you serve their gods, it will surely be a snare to you.

translation_notes: Justice commands resist crowd pressure, false reports, bribery, and bending justice either against or toward poor by partiality.
Enemy animal laws interrupt hatred with concrete help.
Sabbath year/week extends rest to poor, animals, servant's son, and sojourner; liberation becomes ecological/social rhythm.
Sojourner ethics are grounded in knowing the soul/life of the sojourner from Egypt memory.
Land/displacement language is ethically difficult and requires later audit; preserve source wording without using it to justify modern dispossession.
safety_note: Do not use Exodus 23 land language to justify modern ethnic cleansing, colonialism, or religious violence. Covenant faithfulness, justice, sojourner protection, rest, and anti-idolatry must govern interpretation, with conquest/displacement texts flagged for careful ethical audit.
